methods.name: DIFFCOT
methods.description: Diffusion-styled Chain-of-Thought framework that reformulates CoT reasoning as an iterative denoising process with sliding-window mechanism

problems.name: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
problems.description: Step-by-step intermediate reasoning for mathematical problem-solving in LLMs

problems.name: Error Accumulation in CoT
problems.description: Early mistakes propagating irreversibly through autoregressive decoding steps

problems.name: Exposure Bias in CoT
problems.description: Model trained only on correct prefixes but inference may condition on erroneous intermediate steps

relations.subject_name: DIFFCOT: Diffusion-styled Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in LLMs
relations.evidence: we propose Diffusion-styled Chain of Thought (DIFFCOT), which models CoT reasoning in a diffusion-styled framework

relations.subject_name: DIFFCOT: Diffusion-styled Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in LLMs
relations.evidence: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning improves multi-step mathematical problem solving in large language models

relations.subject_name: DIFFCOT: Diffusion-styled Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in LLMs
relations.evidence: mistakes introduced in the early stages can propagate through later steps, often leading to incorrect final answers

relations.subject_name: DIFFCOT: Diffusion-styled Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in LLMs
relations.evidence: the model is exposed only to correct prefixes during training, whereas at inference time the prefixes may contain errors that accumulate across steps, resulting in exposure bias
intermediate steps are not fixed once generated but can be adaptively corrected in light of later context
our method jointly models all reasoning steps within a diffusion paradigm, enabling robust reasoning under realistic
